title: Euonymus subtrinervis
taxonomy: Euonymus subtrinervis is classified within the family Celastraceae. It belongs to the genus Euonymus, which comprises about 60 species of deciduous and evergreen shrubs and small trees. The specific epithet 'subtrinervis' refers to the leaves having a three-nerved leaflet. Euonymus subtrinervis is native to China and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and over-exploitation. The plant is also known as the 'three-nerved spindle tree' due to its unique leaf characteristic.
uses: Euonymus subtrinervis has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is believed to have various health benefits. The plant's extracts are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential interactions with other treatments.

side_effects: Euonymus subtrinervis is not commonly known for its medicinal or edible uses, and therefore, there is limited information available on potential side effects. intro: Euonymus subtrinervis is a plant species commonly known as the Japanese spindle or three-veined spindle, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, suggesting its use in treating conditions such as fever, skin infections, and gastrointestinal issues. Euonymus subtrinervis contains bioactive constituents like alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional medicine in East Asia, particularly in China and Japan.
